'''Scott Dureau''' (born 29 July 1986) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. A [[Goal (sport)#Rugby football|goal]]-kicking {{rlp|hb}}, he played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Background==
Dureau was born in [[Taree, New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
===Newcastle Knights===
[[File:Scott Dureau.jpg|thumb|left|165px|Dureau playing for the Knights in 2009]]As a young man, Dureau played his junior football for the [[Port Macquarie]] Sharks. In the early 2000s, the [[Newcastle Knights]] signed him on a deal that would see him playing in the lower grades. He won various awards at the club before making his debut against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] at [[Bluetongue Stadium]] in 2007.

In July 2010, Dureau was told by Knights coach [[Rick Stone]] that it would be in Dureau's best interests to find a new club for 2011, after the signing of young playmaker [[Beau Henry]] and the re-signing of [[Ben Rogers (rugby league)|Ben Rogers]], plus players [[Jarrod Mullen]] and [[Kurt Gidley]] that also played in the halves.<ref>{{cite news|work=The Herald|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/457299/dureau-vows-top-grade-finish-to-knights-career/|title=Dureau vows top-grade finish to Knights career|first=Robert|last=Dillon|date=8 July 2010}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons ===
Dureau signed with the [[Catalans Dragons]] for 2011.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.nbntv.com.au/index.php/2010/08/12/france-bound-dureau-replaces-mullen-for-clash-with-warriors/ |title=France-Bound Dureau Replaces Mullen for Clash with Warriors : NBN |accessdate=2010-08-12 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110404174503/http://www.nbntv.com.au/index.php/2010/08/12/france-bound-dureau-replaces-mullen-for-clash-with-warriors/ |archivedate=4 April 2011 }}</ref> He started out at Catalans as the first choice halfback and played for three years with much individual and team success. He was named in the [[Super League Dream Team]] in 2011 and 2012, and in 2012 he was awarded the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]].<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|accessdate=16 February 2014}}</ref>

Dureau missed much of the 2013 and 2014 seasons due to surgery to remove a benign tumour behind his eye.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/21703038|title=Scott Dureau: Eye tumour diagnosis was 'scary and intense'|publisher=BBC Sport|date=7 March 2013|accessdate=6 November 2018}}</ref>

===Sydney Roosters===
On 30 June 2014, Dureau signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]] mid-season on loan from the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.roosters.com.au/news/2014/06/30/roosters_sign_dureau_stapleton_for_remainder_of_2014.html|title=Roosters sign Dureau & Stapleton for remainder of 2014|date=30 June 2014|accessdate=6 November 2018}}</ref> However he failed to make an NRL appearance for the club.

===Return to Catalans===
In 2015, Dureau returned to the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.sportchoc.tv/sport/article/dragons-catalans/10485-willie-tonga-todd-carney-est-une-star |title=Dragons Catalans Willie Tonga : "Todd Carney est une star" | SportChoc.tv |access-date=17 November 2014 |archive-date=29 November 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141129022310/http://www.sportchoc.tv/sport/article/dragons-catalans/10485-willie-tonga-todd-carney-est-une-star |url-status=dead }}</ref> Dureau left the club at the end of the 2015 season and subsequently retired from the sport.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33469525|title=Scott Dureau: Catalans Dragons half-back to exit at end of season|publisher=BBC Sport|date=9 July 2015|accessdate=6 November 2018}}</ref>

==Coaching career==
Following his retirement as a player, Dureau took up a coaching role with Newcastle Knights.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_20438-dureau-hangs-up-his-boots.html|title=Dureau hangs up his boots|accessdate=6 November 2018}}</ref>
